video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 17
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday July 17th: Trump questions legitimacy of U.S. elections; U.S. strikes bridges in Iran; Democrats in Congress have questions about ICE training; Smoke making air dangerous in U.S.

video:video.video:title: Alzheimer's blood test may predict earliest stage of disease but doctors warn it lacks precision
video:video.video:description: A blood test may predict the start of Alzheimer's symptoms years in advance and doctors say it could become a game changer.

video:video.video:title: Landslide in southwest China buries homes and forces hundreds to evacuate
video:video.video:description: A landslide Friday in the southwestern Chinese city of Chongqing buried residential buildings, forced more than 1,100 residents to evacuate and trapped an undetermined number of people, state media reported.

video:video.video:title: Israeli strikes cause massive damage to areas of Gaza it told people to leave
video:video.video:description: Israel ordered Palestinians in four major areas of the central Gaza Strip to evacuate before intense airstrikes Wednesday night that residents said heavily damaged homes they had returned to in hopes of rebuilding their lives during a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.

video:video.video:title: How an Italian sculptor created the World Cup trophy that became an icon
video:video.video:description: The Italian sculptor who designed the FIFA World Cup trophy wanted to capture three sporting emotions in a single spiralling form: the athlete’s struggle, the fan’s jubilation and the moment of victory.

video:video.video:title: Trump uses primetime address to raise doubts about US elections ahead of midterms
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump used a primetime address to the nation Thursday to elevate his yearslong push to raise doubts about the legitimacy of U.S. elections and dispute his 2020 loss — this time, to justify his push to pass a strict voter ID bill.

video:video.video:title: Who is Andy Burnham? The man who went from Manchester mayor to Britain's next prime minister
video:video.video:description: Andy Burnham got to the top through a mix of patience and risk-taking. A decade ago, Burnham abandoned a 20-year climb up the Labour Party ladder in London to head north and run for mayor of Greater Manchester.

video:video.video:title: Burnham declared leader of UK's Labour Party and will become prime minister on Monday
video:video.video:description: Andy Burnham was officially declared leader of Britain’s governing Labour Party on Friday. The former mayor of Greater Manchester was the only contender in the center-left party’s leadership contest to replace departing Prime Minister Keir Starmer, who was forced out by a Labour rebellion.

video:video.video:title: Wildfire smoke kills tens of thousands of people a year. Here's why and what you can do about it
video:video.video:description: Smoke from wildfires — which are burning more of Northern Hemisphere as Earth warms — attacks nearly every system in the human body, killing tens of thousands of people a year.

video:video.video:title: Modi inaugurates India's first domestically built, hydrogen-powered train
video:video.video:description: The train will operate in the northern state of Haryana and can run at speeds of up to 75 kph (47 mph) and carry about 2,600 passengers, railway officials said.

video:video.video:title: What are earthquakes and why do they happen?
video:video.video:description: Earthquakes typically occur along edges of tectonic plates. But their impacts may be felt in a broader region. (AP video produced by Javier Arciga)

video:video.video:title: Burnham officially declared leader of UK's Labour Party and will become prime minister on Monday
video:video.video:description: Andy Burnham was officially declared leader of Britain's governing Labour Party on Friday, promising to bring hope to the British people and purpose to the floundering government as he cleared his final hurdle to take office as prime minister next week.

video:video.video:title: Wildfire smoke kills tens of thousands of people a year. Here's why and what you can do about it
video:video.video:description: Smoke from wildfires — which are burning more of the Northern Hemisphere as Earth warms — attacks nearly every system in the human body, killing tens of thousands of people a year, medical studies show.

video:video.video:title: Powerful quake off southern Mexico rattles Guatemala and Central America
video:video.video:description: People evacuated buildings in Guatemala City on Friday after a strong earthquake struck off Mexico’s southern Pacific coast near the border with Guatemala.

video:video.video:title: Partner of man killed by ICE in Maine remembers him as loving, hardworking and devoted to his family
video:video.video:description: Karolina Rojas remembered her partner, Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero, who was shot dead by federal immigration agents in Maine, as a loving, hardworking man who did everything to make her and their daughter happy.

video:video.video:title: China's world AI conference opens with humanoid robots
video:video.video:description: Humanoid robots have emerged as the biggest attraction on the opening day of the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC) on Friday.

video:video.video:title: Landslide in southwest China buries homes and forces hundreds to evacuate
video:video.video:description: A landslide Friday in the southwestern Chinese city of Chongqing buried residential buildings, forced more than 1,100 residents to evacuate and trapped an undetermined number of people, state media reported.

video:video.video:title: The story behind Messi's iconic photo with baby Lamine Yamal
video:video.video:description: Long before they shared the World Cup stage, Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal shared a photo. Photographer Joan Monfort captured the now-iconic image nearly 19 years ago during a charity shoot at Barcelona's Camp Nou, when Messi posed with baby Yamal.

video:video.video:title: A Brazilian bay’s surprise shark nursery sparks conservation project
video:video.video:description: A Brazilian bay’s surprise shark nursery sparks conservation project

video:video.video:title: Partial touch and movement returns to paralysed man following brain implant
video:video.video:description: A man completely paralysed in a diving accident has been given a lasting sensation of touch and partial movement after a double neural bypass operation.

video:video.video:title: What happened after WHAM! played China?
video:video.video:description: New documentary "WHAM! 10 Days in China" highlights not just how the concerts helped George Michael and Andrew Ridgeley to make it big, but the impact it had on the Chinese audience.

video:video.video:title: World Cup beer sales are hopping. Brewers hope the stout demand outlasts the soccer tournament
video:video.video:description: The Boston Beer Company's Tap Room had to make emergency deliveries, tap their kegs and drain two brewery tank to keep up with demand from their World Cup customers.

video:video.video:title: Latin American governments prepare for El Nino as drought, floods and heat loom
video:video.video:description: Latin American governments prepare for El Nino as drought, floods and heat loom

video:video.video:title: Reading glasses transform the work and lives of Bangladesh garment workers
video:video.video:description: Glasses are helping to boost productivity and quality of life for workers in some Bangladesh garment factories.

video:video.video:title: DNA from bones on a Revolutionary War battlefield solves the case of 'America's oldest John Doe'
video:video.video:description: For more than two centuries, the Revolutionary War soldier was unknown and unnoticed. Now, thanks to happenstance and the wonders of DNA, he has a name, just in time for the 250th birthday of the nation he fought and died to create.

video:video.video:title: Wildfires burn in Spain as country battles multiple blazes
video:video.video:description: Multiple wildfires were burning across Spain on Friday, authorities said, including in the Aragon and Madrid regions.

video:video.video:title: Three dead in about a week from ICE encounters
video:video.video:description: In roughly a week, three people have been killed related to encounters with ICE. This is what we know so far about the cases.

video:video.video:title: US releases footage depicting strike on key Iran surveillance tower
video:video.video:description: The United States expanded its airstrike campaign against Iran early Friday by hitting more bridges, energy sites and collapsing a tower at a key Iranian port.

video:video.video:title: United States launches strikes against Iran as battle over the Strait of Hormuz intensifies
video:video.video:description: The United States and Iran exchanged strikes aimed at infrastructure and military targets on Saturday as their battle over the Strait of Hormuz intensified. The U.S.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers at the scene after deadly landslide in China's Chongqing
video:video.video:description: A landslide Friday on the outskirts of the southwestern Chinese city of Chongqing caused deaths and left dozens missing, burying residential buildings and forcing more than 1,100 people to evacuate, according to local officials and state media reports.

video:video.video:title: Families in Venezuela seek closure as they search for loved ones' remains after twin earthquakes
video:video.video:description: More than three weeks after devastating earthquakes struck Venezuela, families continued the desperate search to recover the bodies of loved ones from the rubble of collapsed buildings.

video:video.video:title: Women are dying in Africa as US ramps up its global battle against abortion
video:video.video:description: Women are dying in Africa as US ramps up its global battle against abortion

video:video.video:title: Puerto Ricans react to announcement of emergency water rationing as drought worsens
video:video.video:description: Water rationing measures were set to take effect in several communities in Puerto Rico on Friday as a drought grips the U.S. territory.

video:video.video:title: Sirens and explosions in Kuwait City as Iran and US escalate strikes
video:video.video:description: Kuwait announced on Saturday that its air defense systems had intercepted and downed Iranian missiles launched at the tiny Gulf State.

video:video.video:title: Lettuce at Taco Bell is linked to diarrhea-causing parasite outbreak in 5 states
video:video.video:description: Federal health officials have identified lettuce from Mexico served at Taco Bell locations across five U.S. states as a source of the widespread outbreak of diarrhea-causing parasite cyclospora.

video:video.video:title: Two men say they were switched as infants and are suing a North Dakota hospital
video:video.video:description: Kyle Bylin and Jeremy Morrison were both born on Jan. 26, 1988, at Unity Medical Center in Grafton, North Dakota.

video:video.video:title: AP Explains: ICE officer in Maine shooting has history of violent behavior, family and records say
video:video.video:description: The ICE officer who shot a Colombian man in Maine this week is an Army veteran who has struggled with serious mental health issues since early childhood, according to close relatives who spoke to The Associated Press.

video:video.video:title: China's Xpeng debuts flying car in Germany
video:video.video:description: The Chinese manufacturer's 'Land Aircraft Carrier' carries a module in the back which can fly two people through the air. (AP video: Philipp Jenne)

video:video.video:title: Texas floods leave homes muddy and roads flooded
video:video.video:description: First responders in Texas rescued people trapped in dangerously rising flood waters in the southern area of the state Friday after days of punishing downpours created floods that killed two people.

video:video.video:title: Clara Ester, activist who rushed to Martin Luther King Jr. after he was shot, dies at 78
video:video.video:description: Clara Ester, an activist and minister who rushed to the Rev. Martin Luther King Jr.'s side when he was shot, has died at 78. (AP Video: Kristin M. Hall)

video:video.video:title: Fanatics Fest fans reveal the craziest things they’ve done for sports
video:video.video:description: How far would you go for sports? We asked fans at Fanatics Fest to share their most unforgettable fan stories.

video:video.video:title: Shamans gather in Lima and predict an Argentina World Cup victory
video:video.video:description: Shamans from various regions of Peru, Bolivia and Venezuela gathered outside a stadium in Lima on Friday to perform a ritual sending support to both Spain and Argentina ahead of the World Cup final.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 17
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, July 17th: Two dead in Texas flooding; Dems demand answers about DHS vetting of ICE officers; Sen.

video:video.video:title: Brad Paisley shares details of Taylor Swift’s wedding invite
video:video.video:description: Brad Paisley says Taylor Swift’s wedding invite made him feel deeply appreciated, calling the celebration one of the least pretentious he’s ever attended.

video:video.video:title: Trump's envoy faces protests in Venice on latest stop of super yacht diplomacy tour
video:video.video:description: Protesters in Venice have clashed with police over the presence of a luxury yacht owned by U.S. ambassador Tilman Fertitta.

video:video.video:title: Texas floods have left high waters and a big cleanup job after hundreds of people are rescued
video:video.video:description: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott has requested a Presidential Disaster Declaration to aid in the state's response to severe weather and flooding affecting communities statewide.

video:video.video:title: Brad Paisley says 'Tacklebox' honors classic country sound
video:video.video:description: Brad Paisley hopes 'Tacklebox' finds a home in fans’ playlists. He says the project captures the traditional country sound of the ’90s and early 2000s, and hopes it connects with listeners seeking that classic style.

video:video.video:title: Local Chinese residents join the landslide relief effort in Chongqing
video:video.video:description: The landslide occurred in Pengshui County on Friday morning on the outskirts of Chongqing municipality, when massive amounts of rocks and soil washed down a slope, burying more than 10 residential buildings, state broadcaster CCTV said.

video:video.video:title: How the Strait of Hormuz is monitored as US-Iran conflict escalates
video:video.video:description: The United States intensified its strikes on Iran early Thursday, hitting targets further north as American forces also fired into a ship the U.S. accused of trying to break its naval blockade on the Islamic Republic.

video:video.video:title: Thousands attend funeral for Pakistani police officers killed by insurgents in Quetta
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people attended funeral prayers in Quetta on Saturday for Pakistani police officers killed by insurgents last week.

video:video.video:title: Trial of roadside virtual fence tech to reduce vehicle collisions with wildlife
video:video.video:description: The local government in the Australian city of Canberra once again faced calls to explore a trial of roadside "virtual fence" technology to reduce vehicle collisions with wildlife.

video:video.video:title: Fans unfurl giant jersey of Argentina's Lionel Messi in his hometown
video:video.video:description: Fans unfurled a giant Lionel Messi jersey in the Argentine city of Rosario on Saturday, thanking the soccer star ahead of the World Cup final.

video:video.video:title: River Danube in Romania reaches record low flow in 30 years
video:video.video:description: The Danube River fell to its lowest flow in 30 years on Thursday and continued to drop through Saturday, with the downward trend expected to hold at least until Monday, Romania's National Waters Administration said.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 18
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, July 18th: US military says 2 troops are dead and 1 missing after Iran attacks; Ukrainian drones hit Russian warehouses; Massive fire becomes Norway's largest in modern times; Argentina and Spanish fans ready for final.

video:video.video:title: Brad Paisley on Taylor Swift’s wedding
video:video.video:description: Brad Paisley says Taylor Swift’s wedding was one of the least pretentious he has attended or will ever see.

video:video.video:title: D-Day landing beaches bid for UNESCO World Heritage status
video:video.video:description: The beaches where Allied forces landed for the D-Day operation could be awarded UNESCO World Heritage Status.

video:video.video:title: Greece’s Mount Olympus vies for UNESCO world heritage site status
video:video.video:description: Mount Olympus, the mythological home of ancient Greece’s 12 Olympian gods, may soon join UNESCO’s World Heritage List.

video:video.video:title: New footage of Monaco bombing released by Ukraine's general prosecutor
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian prosecutors have released what they say is video of the explosion in Monaco that seriously injured tycoon Vadym Yermolaiev and his family.

video:video.video:title: World Cup beer sales are hopping. Brewers hope the stout demand outlasts the soccer tournament
video:video.video:description: The World Cup has been a bonanza for beer. Bars in Boston needed emergency deliveries after some taps ran dry. (AP Video by Rodrique Ngowi)

video:video.video:title: Massive fire becomes Norway's largest in modern times after destroying more than 100 homes
video:video.video:description: The largest residential fire in modern Norwegian history destroyed more than 100 homes and forced hundreds of people to evacuate, authorities said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Inside Tasmania's newest library, home to a copy of Shakespeare's First Folio
video:video.video:description: As this year's Dark Mofo art festival came to an end, the Museum of Old and New Art (MONA) in Hobart unveiled its largest expansion since opening 15 years ago.

video:video.video:title: Women amputees in South America train to be World Cup soccer contenders
video:video.video:description: Women amputees from several countries are training in Bolivia for the 2027 Women's Amputee Football World Cup.

video:video.video:title: As war roils in Iran, health workers in Nigeria says more children are relapsing into malnutrition
video:video.video:description: Health workers in northern Nigeria say more children are relapsing into malnutrition and cite knock-on effects from the war in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Buildings damaged in Kyiv after authorities report an overnight Russian attack
video:video.video:description: One person was killed and eight others injured in an overnight Russian attack on Kyiv that included ballistic missiles, local authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters struggle to control huge Zaragoza wildfire in Spain
video:video.video:description: The fire in "Cinco Villas", in the province of Zaragoza, remained out of control on Saturday, as shown by images captured by drones from the Civil Guard’s Pegaso team.

video:video.video:title: Bridge near Bandar Abbas severely damaged in latest US strikes
video:video.video:description: Overnight U.S. strikes damaged a bridge and two tunnels, disrupting one of the main highways towards Bandar Abbas, a city which sits near the narrowest part of the Strait of Hormuz, according to Iran's state-run news agency.

video:video.video:title: US military ends latest airstrikes on Iran
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military launched airstrikes Sunday targeting Iran's paramilitary Revolutionary Guard to retaliate for the killing of American troops in Jordan, further widening the crossfire between the nations as they fight over Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Trump and Infantino declare World Cup a success ahead of Argentina vs Spain final
video:video.video:description: FIFA President Gianni Infantino on Friday declared the World Cup a success, praising President Donald Trump for his role in the tournament and saying it had fulfilled its mission of bringing people together from around the world.
